content:
# GPU Inference Saturation вҖ” Fused RAUNE+Depth Batch

## Data flow: before vs. after

**Before (two separate batch calls, depth on original):**
```
Rust в”Җв”Җoriginal_pxв”Җв”Җв–ә IPC в”Җв”Җв–ә RAUNE в”Җв”Җв–ә IPC в”Җв”Җв–ә Rust (enhanced_px stored)
Rust в”Җв”Җoriginal_pxв”Җв”Җв–ә IPC в”Җв”Җв–ә Depth в”Җв”Җв–ә IPC в”Җв”Җв–ә Rust (depths stored)
         ^в”Җв”Җ 4 IPC data crossings, enhanced_px goes GPUвҶ’CPUвҶ’pipeвҶ’CPUвҶ’GPU for nothing
```

**After (fused call, depth on RAUNE output):**
```
Rust в”Җв”Җoriginal_pxв”Җв”Җв–ә IPC в”Җв”Җв–ә RAUNE в”Җв”Җresize+renorm(GPU)в”Җв”Җв–ә Depth в”Җв”Җв–ә IPC в”Җв”Җв–ә Rust
         ^в”Җв”Җ 2 IPC data crossings, enhanced tensor stays on GPU the whole time
```

Saved per 8-frame chunk: one IPC round-trip (~30вҖ“50 ms), one dtoh of enhanced frames, one htod of enhanced frames. Better depth maps as a bonus.

---


## Resize and renormalise between models

RAUNE outputs at `max_size=1024` (no patch constraint). Depth Anything V2 requires dimensions вүӨ 518 that are **multiples of 14** (ViT patch size). Between the two models, on GPU:

```python
import torch.nn.functional as F
